= Commercial (Alternative) Licensing Lane

This repository is primarily licensed under link:LICENSE[GNU Affero General Public License v3.0] (AGPL-3.0).

== Why an alternative license exists

Some organizations want to use CentrMark in proprietary or closed-source products and services without triggering AGPL's corresponding source-sharing obligations.

If we have the necessary rights to do so for the relevant parts of the repository, we may offer an alternative commercial license to those organizations (typically via a license agreement that may include a license fee).

This is a legal licensing lane, not paid support.

== What this does and does not mean

* **Does not** automatically grant additional rights to every reader.
* **Does** provide a path to obtain alternative terms through a written agreement with the appropriate copyright holders.

Until you receive such an agreement in writing, your use is governed only by the AGPL-3.0 license.

== Eligibility and rights

Alternative licensing is only possible for portions where the relevant maintainers/copyright holders control the rights.

If contributions are made that the maintainers cannot relicense, those contributions may remain AGPL-3.0-only.

== Process (high level)

1. Contact the maintainers with a short description of your intended use (product type, distribution model, whether it is network-facing, and whether you need the AGPL obligations avoided).
2. The maintainers confirm whether alternative licensing is available for the required parts.
3. If available, the maintainers provide a commercial license agreement for signature and return you a copy of the executed terms.

== How this helps the standard

The goal is to route meaningful funding and legal clarity into the canonical CentrMark effort while still keeping the baseline standard open and forkable.
